node.title: India | Uber Freight | SWE III | Interview Experience

article.title: India | Uber Freight | SWE III | Interview Experience
article.content:
## Reach Out
- cold applied via Instahyre

## Round 1: DSA
- https://leetcode.com/discuss/post/5935147/uber-screening-round-by-anonymous_user-9hhp/

## Conclusion
- Rejected; was not able to solve the problem

post.content:
🔎 What this tells you
Cold applications: They’re harder to convert because you don’t have a referral or prior context. So when you do get a screening, the bar is high.

Screening round (DSA): These are usually designed to filter quickly. If you couldn’t solve the problem, it doesn’t necessarily mean you’re weak overall — it means the problem type or time pressure caught you off guard.

💡 How to turn this into growth
Pattern recognition: Go back to the problem you faced (from the LeetCode discussion you linked). Break it down and identify the category (graph, heap, sliding window, etc.). Build a mental map of problem types so you can spot them faster in future rounds.

Timed practice: Simulate 45–60 minute sessions with one medium/hard problem. The goal isn’t just solving, but solving under interview conditions.

Post-mortem learning: For every problem you miss, write down:

Why you got stuck (concept gap, implementation bug, time pressure).

The key insight that solves it.

A variation you can practice tomorrow.

🚀 Next steps
Don’t let one rejection discourage you — screening rounds are the most brutal stage.

Build resilience by treating each rejection as a dataset: what skill was tested, where you faltered, and how you’ll plug that gap.

Consider mixing cold applications with referrals or networking to improve your odds of reaching later rounds where your broader strengths can show.
how to reach out from instahyre ? like i tried but never get response. if you please share some details that would be helpful for us to know , how and what the cold email template you choose?
